description:
You are a security engineer performing a quarterly credential-rotation on a small set of service accounts.  
Everything takes place inside the non-privileged user’s home directory (/home/user).  

PRE-EXISTING MATERIAL  
1. Directory: /home/user/secops/  
2. File: /home/user/secops/credentials_2023-12-01.csv – CSV with a header row followed by three credential records.  
   Current content (verify it’s there but **do not change it in-place**):  
       username,service,credential_id,created_at,expires_at  
       alice,db,credA1,2023-12-01,2024-06-01  
       bob,web,credB2,2023-12-01,2024-06-01  
       carol,api,credC3,2023-12-01,2024-06-01  

GOAL  
Rotate those credentials and produce three deliverables while leaving the original CSV intact:  

A. A point-in-time backup of the original file inside /home/user/secops/archive/ named credentials_2023-12-01.csv.bak (exact filename).  
   • The backup must be byte-for-byte identical to the original.  

B. A new credential file /home/user/secops/credentials_2024-06-01.csv that meets ALL of the following rules:  
   • Uses the exact same header row as the source.  
   • For every data row:  
     - created_at must be replaced with the rotation date 2024-06-01.  
     - expires_at must become 2024-12-01.  
     - credential_id must receive the suffix “-R1” (dash, capital R, digit one) appended to the existing value.  
   • Lines must be terminated with a single Unix newline character (\n).  
   • File must contain precisely four rows (header + 3 data lines).  

   Example of ONE transformed line (for illustration, yours must follow the pattern for all users):  
       alice,db,credA1-R1,2024-06-01,2024-12-01  

C. A text log /home/user/secops/rotation_logs/rotate_2024-06-01.log capturing the rotation activity with the following STRICT three-line format (no extra spaces, tabs, or blank lines):  
       ROTATION_COMPLETE  
       DATE: 2024-06-01  
       TOTAL: 3  

VERIFICATION NOTES  
• Paths and filenames must match exactly as written.  
• The automated checker will confirm:  
  – existence and SHA256 match of the backup file,  
  – correct contents of credentials_2024-06-01.csv (header + the three transformed rows in their original order),  
  – exact three-line content of rotate_2024-06-01.log.  
• You do not have root; create any missing directories with normal permissions.  

Typical workflow will require roughly 5-10 shell commands (mkdir, cp, awk/sed/cut, echo/printf, etc.).  
Make sure you finish by printing or otherwise leaving the files in place; console output is fine, but only the on-disk artefacts are graded.
truth:
Pre-created structure before the agent starts:

Directory: /home/user/secops/

File: /home/user/secops/credentials_2023-12-01.csv  
Exact bytes (including trailing newline after each line):
username,service,credential_id,created_at,expires_at\n
alice,db,credA1,2023-12-01,2024-06-01\n
bob,web,credB2,2023-12-01,2024-06-01\n
carol,api,credC3,2023-12-01,2024-06-01\n

No archive/ or rotation_logs/ directories exist yet.

After correct completion the following must additionally exist:

1. /home/user/secops/archive/credentials_2023-12-01.csv.bak  
   – File SHA256 must equal the SHA256 of the original credentials_2023-12-01.csv.

2. /home/user/secops/credentials_2024-06-01.csv  
   – Expected exact content (each line terminated with \n, no extra blank lines):

username,service,credential_id,created_at,expires_at\n
alice,db,credA1-R1,2024-06-01,2024-12-01\n
bob,web,credB2-R1,2024-06-01,2024-12-01\n
carol,api,credC3-R1,2024-06-01,2024-12-01\n

3. /home/user/secops/rotation_logs/rotate_2024-06-01.log  
   – Exact three-line content (each with trailing newline):

ROTATION_COMPLETE\n
DATE: 2024-06-01\n
TOTAL: 3\n
